items.title: Decision tree feature selection
items.description: Algorithms like ID3 and C4.5 choose splits that maximize information gain, defined as the reduction in label entropy H(Y) after partitioning on a feature. Entropy quantifies how much each candidate split reduces class uncertainty.

items.title: Classification loss and cross-entropy
items.description: Cross-entropy between true labels and model predictions decomposes into H(Y) plus a KL term. Minimizing cross-entropy pushes predicted distributions toward the data; understanding H(Y) clarifies the irreducible baseline loss from label uncertainty.

items.title: Language model evaluation (perplexity)
items.description: Perplexity exp(H) reports the effective number of choices a language model faces per token. Lower cross-entropy on held-out text means lower entropy relative to the data-generating process and better predictive compression.

items.title: Data compression and coding
items.description: Shannon's source coding theorem shows no lossless code can average fewer than H(X) bits per symbol for source X. Huffman and arithmetic codes approach this limit when matched to the true PMF.
